title: Jamesbrittenia acutiloba
what_description: Jamesbrittenia acutiloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is an annual or short-lived perennial herb with a slender stem that reaches up to 30 cm in height, and has linear-lanceolate leaves that are 2-5 cm long and 0.3-1 cm wide. The leaves are arranged oppositely and are hairy on both surfaces. The flowers are small, tubular, and purple in color, with a short tube and a 4-lobed corolla that is 0.8-1.2 cm long. The plant is endemic to the Western Cape Province of South Africa and typically grows in moist areas.

intro: Jamesbrittenia acutiloba, also known as the "thin-lobed Jamesbrittenia", is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of South Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in the treatment of various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of Jamesbrittenia acutiloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
